sutta: once the exalted 1 was dwelling near savathi in eastern pong at the terrace house of megara's mother now at that time the exalted 1 was seated surrounded by monks of the sangha the 8th day of the lunar month the 15th day 23rd day and the last day of the lunar month and on these days and the people in india would keep the 8th precepts and on 2 of these days on the 15th and the last day of the luna calendar the monks will assemble to recite the patimoka the precepts the code or precepts of the monks then the suta continues then when the night was far spent and the first watch was gone by arose from his seat and placing his upper robe over 1 shoulder bent his hands to the exalted 1 and said lord the night is far spent the first watch is gone by and long have the monks of the sangha been seated lord let the exalted 1 recite the patimoka to the monks thus spoke the venable ananda but the exalted 1 remains silent stop here for a moment uh from this we can realize that um in the initial days of the sangha the buddha used to recite the buddha was the 1 who recited the moka to the monks and we can see from this utah later why the buddha stopped reciting and later the buddha asked the senior most manga among the sangha it was the duty of the senior most monk to recite the patamoka but sometimes when the senior most monk did not want to recite he will nominate another monk to recite to to chant the pati mokka and here the first watch had had ended and the buddha had still not recited the pati moka the first watch is from 6 pm to 10 pm and the buddha had still not recited the patamoka that is why that was why the venable ananda reminded the buddha to recite the patimoka but the buddha kept quiet and the suta continues and the verbal ananda when the night was far spent and the middle watch was gone by arose from his seat a second time and bent his hands to the exalted 1 and said lord the night is far spent the middle watch is gone by and long have the monks been seated lord let the exalted 1 recite the patimoka to the monks the second time even the exalted 1 remains silent i'll stop for a moment to commend that the middle watcher is from 10 pm until 2 a.m so after 2 a.m the the monks were seated and still the buddha did not reside the third watch is from 2 am until 6 00 am and then the suta continues then when the night was passed when the last watch was gone by that means the third watch has was gone by but the sun was getting up gladdening the face of night the third time the rainbow ananda arose from his seat and said to the exalted 1 lord the night is past the last watch is gone by and the sun rises and the face of night brightens lord long have the monks been sitting let the exalted 1 recite the patamoka to the monks and the buddha said ananda the assembly is not real then the venerable maha muglana thought of what person i wonder does the exalted 1 say the assembly ananda is not pure and he fixed his thought intently upon all the monks of the sangha there encompassing their minds with his now the vulnerable maha muglana saw a person seated in the midst of the monks who was wicked evil unclean of suspicious conduct full of secret actions no recruits go vowed there to unchase no vow to chastity rotten to the core lustful and vile and rising from his seat he approached him and said get up sir the exalted 1 has seen you for you there is no fellowship with the monks but when he had dust broken that person remained silent the second time he addressed him in like manner and the second time the third the person was silent a third time he said get up sir the exalted 1 has seen you there is no fellowship for you with the monks but the third time that person was silent at that the verbal maha muglana sees the man by the arm and thrusts him outside the porch and voted the door then the verbal muhammad went to the exalted 1 and said lord that person has been expelled by me the assembly is pure lord let the exalted 1 recite the patimoka and the buddha said it is very strange mawlana that that foolish person should have waited till he was seized by the arm then spoke the exalted 1 to the monk saying now months may you keep the upper sata now may you recite the patimoka henceforth from today i shall not recite the patamoka for it is impossible monks nor can it happen that the tata gata should recite the patamoka to an assembly which is not perfectly pure that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so uh you can see here in the earliest days of the sangha the buddha recited the party moka for the monks but later as more and more monks ordained many of them the earliest monks were ordained personally ordained by the buddha but as more and more people joined the sangha then it became impossible for the buddha to ordain everybody so he allowed his disciples to ordain other disciples and when that had happened many in unsincere people joined the sangha shameless people just like this 1 you can see this person was so shameless even though the buddha could read his mind and probably knew that the buddha recognize him as a shameless monk and still he refused to leave the assembly and even when the maha mughal came to ask him to leave he still refused to leave even after 3 times and memorable mahatma gana had to catch him by the arm and throw him outside the door uh and we see also in another suta later in the utah nikaya in 1 of the latest the arahan maha kasapa was asked by the buddha to teach dhamma to the monks and mahaka sapa also knew that there were a lot of insincere monks in the assembly and he refused to teach dhamma to the monks and he told the buddha the reason and the buddha agreed with him he noticed here after the rotten monk was thrown outside the the porch the member maha mubarak bolted the door and in the earliest days under patimoca the precepts used to be recited in the couti on the hardness of the senior most monk and the sangha consisted of all monks in the boundary of sema and in the earliest days uh the boundary of shima used to be a very large area but because of rotten monks like these the sima the the boundary became smaller and smaller to exclude these rotten monks until nowadays uh the boundary or the sema consists of a just a building a hall a building to so that when the monks are assembled in the in the sema hall then only those monks inside the sima hall are considered to be part of the sangha and the next suta is
